perturbation.original_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersede any prior oral or written understandings between the parties relating to confidential treatment of information.
perturbation.changed_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, except for any implied or unwritten agreements, which shall take precedence over the written terms.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a major contradiction to the 'entire agreement' clause. Originally, the written agreement was meant to be the complete and final understanding between the parties. Now, 'implied or unwritten agreements' are given precedence, which directly contradicts the purpose of having a written agreement and introduces significant ambiguity about what terms are actually binding. This undermines the enforceability of the written contract and creates potential for disputes based on subjective interpretations of unwritten agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, except for any implied or unwritten agreements, which shall take precedence over the written terms. Sections 1, 2, 4, 7, 10 and 11 of these Confidentiality Terms and Conditions shall survive any expiration or termination of the License Agreement. 20 [***] = CERTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT, MARKED BY BRACKETS, HAS BEEN OMITTED BECAUSE THE INFORMATION (I) IS NOT MATERIAL AND (II) WOULD BE COMPETITIVELY HARMFUL IF PUBLICLY DISCLOSED.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'implied or unwritten agreements' take precedence over the written terms, directly contradicting the original intent of the 'entire agreement' clause. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text because the change created a contradiction to the original text, which had specified that the written agreement contained the entire understanding, thus making this change cause a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Sections 1, 2, 4, 7, 10 and 11 of these Confidentiality Terms and Conditions shall survive any expiration or termination of the License Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Only Sections 1 and 2 of these Confidentiality Terms and Conditions shall survive any expiration or termination of the License Agreement, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at the time of termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original survival clause by drastically reducing the number of sections that remain in effect after termination. Originally, Sections 1, 2, 4, 7, 10, and 11 survived. Now, only Sections 1 and 2 survive, unless a separate written agreement is made. This introduces uncertainty about the continued obligations of the parties after termination and contradicts the initial intent to have certain key sections remain binding regardless of termination. Now section 4 is not surviving the agreement, but it should.
perturbation.contradicted_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, except for any implied or unwritten agreements, which shall take precedence over the written terms. Only Sections 1 and 2 of these Confidentiality Terms and Conditions shall survive any expiration or termination of the License Agreement, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at the time of termination. 20 [***] = CERTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT, MARKED BY BRACKETS, HAS BEEN OMITTED BECAUSE THE INFORMATION (I) IS NOT MATERIAL AND (II) WOULD BE COMPETITIVELY HARMFUL IF PUBLICLY DISCLOSED.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the sections of the confidentiality agreement that survive termination from Sections 1, 2, 4, 7, 10 and 11 to only Sections 1 and 2, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing. The contradicted text contains the changed text, that only Sections 1 and 2 survive. Therefore, before the modification, sections 4, 7, 10 and 11 would survive termination. The change creates an in-text contradiction because the original agreement stipulated that more sections survived, now it is altered that only two do, going against the spirit and text of the original agreement.
